design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate through storybook worlds, pull vegetables/objects from ground, pick up and throw items at enemies to defeat them, collect power-ups, reach end-of-world bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health management (represented by hearts) combined with puzzle-solving around what objects can be thrown and when; exploration of multi-room levels with branching paths
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Family rescue mission in a dream world; empowerment through environmental interaction and creative problem-solving rather than traditional jumping-on-enemies combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate rejection of jump-on-enemies mechanic in favor of grab-throw-interact gameplay; character choice matters mechanically (each of four characters has different speed/strength/jump height tradeoffs); no time limits; vertical and horizontal scrolling rather than purely linear progression

difficulty.lives_system: Three starting lives; one life lost when character health reaches zero or falls off screen
difficulty.continues_system: Up to two continues allowed per game before Game Over
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpoints; each level restart begins from entrance
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection before each stage allows players to adjust difficulty/playstyle; four different mechanical profiles available; magic potions and Sub-space warps provide optional difficulty reduction paths

progression.upgrade_system: Health mushrooms collected in Sub-space increase maximum health capacity; temporary power items (POW Block, stopwatch) provide tactical advantages within levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential world progression; magical potions in each level unlock temporary Sub-space access which contains warp jars that can skip ahead to later worlds
replayability_hooks: Four playable characters with mechanically distinct playstyles encourage multiple playthroughs
Hidden 1-Up mushrooms and Sub-space coin collection rewards exploration
Bonus Chance minigame between levels allows players to gamble collected coins for extra lives
Optional warp access to later worlds provides variable progression paths

world_building_techniques: Thematic level design (desert with quicksand, snowy areas with slippery surfaces)
Character archetypes (Arabian family: Mama, Papa, Imajin, Lina on rescue mission)
Boss encounters tied to world progression and villain Mamu
Dream logic framing device (Subcon as Mario's dream world)

boss_design.philosophy: One boss at end of each of first six worlds; final encounter with main antagonist Wart in World 7; bosses appear to follow world thematic design
notable_bosses: Wart (final boss, main antagonist of Subcon)

faction_indicators: Dream inhabitants of Subcon (victims of Wart's curse)
danger_signals: Environmental hazards themed by world (quicksand, slippery surfaces)
Enemy variety tied to world themes
environmental_storytelling: World themes communicate narrative context (desert, snow, etc.)
Sub-space as reflected alternate dimension mechanic
Jars as magical gateways suggesting fantastical storybook setting
Character selection screen establishes four distinct protagonists with different appearances

sources_used: tcrf: Yume Koujou: Doki Doki Panic - The Cutting Room Floor
wikipedia: Super Mario Bros. 2 (describes Doki Doki Panic as base game)
